steps.title: Identify the plural subject
steps.content: The plural subject “Both Heinrich Böll and Camilo José Cela” requires a possessive for “contributions.”

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement. Rule: a possessive pronoun must match its antecedent in number; contractions cannot serve as possessives.
  • Choice A — it's: contraction of "it is," not a possessive; grammatically wrong in this slot. ✗
  • Choice B — their: plural possessive pronoun; agrees with plural antecedent "Both Heinrich Böll and Camilo José Cela." ✓
  • Choice C — its: singular possessive pronoun; disagrees with plural antecedent. ✗
  • Choice D — they're: contraction of "they are," not a possessive; grammatically wrong in this slot. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
